title: Pre-op pediatric anesthesia documentation
source_review_basis: committed exact-section summaries and evidence records; full guideline documents were not retained for this pass

examination.text: Supports documenting anesthetist assessment, child and parent or carer questions, involvement, physical and psychological preparation, and age-appropriate information without generating care decisions.
examination.rationale: The assessment and information section supports recording child and parent or carer questions, involvement, preparation, and clinician-completed risk discussion.
examination.evidence_extract: Supports documenting anesthetist assessment, child and parent or carer questions, involvement, physical and psychological preparation, and age-appropriate information without generating care decisions.

source.title: Chapter 10: Guidelines for the Provision of Paediatric Anaesthesia Services 2025

exact_section.heading: Recommendation 5.11 and section 9 - anesthetist assessment, questions, involvement, and information
exact_section.locator: official chapter recommendation 5.11 and patient-information section 9

treatment_and_management.text: Supports documenting age, complexity, comorbidity, supervision context, immediate postanesthesia airway-management status, and completed paediatric preassessment.
treatment_and_management.rationale: The current RCoA section supports documenting age, complexity, comorbidity, supervision, completed paediatric preassessment, and immediate recovery context without generating a care plan.
treatment_and_management.evidence_extract: Supports documenting age, complexity, comorbidity, supervision context, immediate postanesthesia airway-management status, and completed paediatric preassessment.

source_limitations: Age, weight, developmental and communication needs, guardian and consent, fasting, recent illness, comorbidities, previous anesthesia, airway, examination, investigations, procedure, technique, medicine, dose, supervision, postoperative destination, and follow-up remain unsupported.
Any legacy item not explicitly mapped to an exact section remains unsupported pending qualified clinician review.
No mapping authorises an automatic diagnosis, differential, investigation, management decision, medicine, dose, procedure, referral, urgency conclusion, discharge decision, or patient instruction.
All prior supported legacy-item mappings were removed because canonical item-level applicability and workflow-specific rationale were not present in explicit repository records.
curation_policy: Direct guideline curation: retain only explicitly mapped content; rewrite only with explicit item mapping; remove unmapped legacy content; add only committed exact-section evidence summaries.
